title: My Pet Cat
summary: A woman talks about her pet cat, Mia. She describes what Mia looks like, what she eats, and what she likes to do every day.
body: I have a cat. Her name is Mia. She is small and orange. She has big green eyes. Mia lives in my house. She sleeps a lot. Every morning she sits on my bed. She likes to look out of the window. She watches the birds outside. Mia eats fish and dry food. She drinks water from a small bowl. She does not like loud noises. She runs away when she is scared. Every evening I play with her. I have a small toy mouse for her. She jumps and runs after it. Mia is a happy cat. I love her very much.

annotations.exampleEn: She has a pet rabbit at home.
The cat has an orange coat.
The cat looked at me with her big green eyes.
Cats sleep a lot — sometimes up to sixteen hours a day.
She looks out of the window and watches the street.
The cat watches the birds from the window.
The vet said dry food is good for cats' teeth.
I filled the bowl with water for the cat.
The dog is afraid of loud noises like thunder.
The cat runs away when she hears a loud sound.
The cat looks scared when a stranger comes.
I bought a toy mouse for my cat to play with.

grammarNotes.title: Present Simple – Describing Habits and Routines
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Bài này dùng thì hiện tại đơn để mô tả thói quen hằng ngày của con mèo. Với chủ ngữ ngôi thứ ba số ít (she, he, it), động từ thêm '-s' hoặc '-es': 'She sleeps', 'She eats', 'She drinks'. Đây là một trong những quy tắc cơ bản nhất của tiếng Anh.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: This passage uses the present simple to describe daily habits. With a third-person singular subject (she, he, it), add '-s' or '-es' to the verb: 'She sleeps', 'She eats', 'She drinks'. This is one of the most fundamental rules in English grammar.

grammarNotes.title: Negative sentences with 'does not'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để phủ định ở ngôi thứ ba số ít trong thì hiện tại đơn, dùng 'does not' (hoặc 'doesn't') trước động từ nguyên thể: 'She does not like loud noises.' Lưu ý: không thêm '-s' vào động từ sau 'does not'. Sai: 'She does not likes...' Đúng: 'She does not like...'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: To make a negative sentence with a third-person singular subject in the present simple, use 'does not' (or 'doesn't') before the base form of the verb: 'She does not like loud noises.' Never add '-s' to the verb after 'does not'. Wrong: 'She does not likes...' Correct: 'She does not like...'

translationVi: Tôi có một con mèo. Tên của nó là Mia. Nó nhỏ và có màu cam. Nó có đôi mắt xanh to. Mia sống trong nhà tôi. Nó ngủ rất nhiều. Mỗi buổi sáng nó ngồi trên giường của tôi. Nó thích nhìn ra ngoài cửa sổ. Nó quan sát những con chim bên ngoài. Mia ăn cá và thức ăn khô. Nó uống nước từ một cái bát nhỏ. Nó không thích tiếng ồn lớn. Nó chạy trốn khi sợ hãi. Mỗi buổi tối tôi chơi với nó. Tôi có một con chuột đồ chơi nhỏ cho nó. Nó nhảy và chạy theo đồ chơi. Mia là một con mèo hạnh phúc. Tôi rất yêu nó.

questions.explanation: The story says 'Her name is Mia.'
questions.hint: Listen for the cat's name at the beginning of the passage.

questions.explanation: The story says 'She is small and orange.'

questions.hint: Where does the cat stay every day?

options: She sits on the narrator's bed

questions.hint: Think about the morning routine described in the passage.

questions.explanation: The story says 'She watches the birds outside.'

The story says 'She does not like loud noises.'

cloze.template: I have a {{b1}}. Her name is Mia. She is small and {{b2}}. She has big green eyes. Mia lives in my house. She {{b3}} a lot. Every morning she sits on my bed. She likes to look out of the window. She watches the {{b4}} outside. Mia eats fish and dry {{b5}}. She drinks water from a small {{b6}}. She does not like loud {{b7}}. She runs away when she is scared. Every evening I {{b8}} with her. I have a small toy mouse for her. She jumps and runs after it. Mia is a {{b9}} cat. I love her very {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: The whole passage is about a cat named Mia.
The passage says Mia is 'small and orange'.
'She sleeps a lot' describes the cat's main daily habit.
The passage says the cat 'watches the birds outside'.
'Dry food' is the second item in Mia's diet.
'A small bowl' is the container for Mia's water.
'Loud noises' are what frighten the cat.
'Every evening I play with her' describes the evening routine.
'Mia is a happy cat' — the narrator describes her pet positively.
'I love her very much' is the closing sentence showing deep affection.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says Mia runs away when she is scared. Why do you think some animals are scared of loud noises? What can people do to help a pet feel safe?
